title: Roman Letters: 7,049 Letters from the Late Roman World (100-800 AD)
description: A structured dataset of 7,049 letters from the late ancient Roman world (100-800 AD), spanning 54 collections and 1,850 identified correspondents. Includes modern English translations (3,068 produced for the first time using AI assistance), geographic coordinates, approximate dates, subject summaries, topic classifications, and network relationship data. Inspired by Patrick Wyman's dissertation 'Letters, Mobility, and the Fall of the Roman Empire' (USC, 2016).
